name: zvid_image_destroy
description: zVid_Image::Destroy releases backing surface state when present, releases owned buffers, frees the image object, and returns zero including for null image input.
known_limits: VC5SP3 cl 11.00.7022 x86 /O2 /Ob0 /Zp4 /FAcs COFF byte comparison for ?Destroy@zVid_Image@@YIHPAUzVidImagePartial@@@Z at 0x46ecc0 still fails: 10 unmasked mismatches, 12 relocation bytes masked, 8 trailing VC NOP bytes trimmed, BN size 41 bytes, VC object symbol size 48 bytes.

Reviewed difference is direct free relocation and branch-label/epilogue padding drift. Source was corrected to match original provider-hook behavior by calling g_zVideo_pfnImageEnsureSurfaceForCurrentDevice whenever the surface field is non-null. The native smoke verifies owned-buffer cleanup through Destroy and null-image return behavior.
